title: Open Problem Record Aging
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:problem" earliest=-180d
| where NOT match(lower(state),"(?i)closed|resolved")
| eval age_days=round((now()-sys_created_on)/86400,0)
| stats count as open_pr, median(age_days) as med_age, max(age_days) as max_age by assignment_group, category
| sort -open_pr
description: Aging open problems indicate stalled root-cause work and undermine permanent corrective actions; backlog visibility strengthens ITIL problem management cadence with incident and change stakeholders.
value: Aging open problems indicate stalled root-cause work and undermine permanent corrective actions; backlog visibility strengthens ITIL problem management cadence with incident and change stakeholders.
implementation: (1) Map `state` values for your problem workflow; (2) Add filters for known backlog categories excluded from SLA; (3) Weekly review of `max_age` with problem managers and link to related `snow:incident` volume via `problem_id` in a companion panel.
visualization: Table (open problem aging by group), Bar chart (open count by category), Single value (total open problems).
knownFalsePositives: Stale or old records while waiting on a vendor, a customer reply, a patch, or work we chose to park on purpose.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:problem"` (`state`, `sys_created_on`, `assignment_group`, `category`, `number`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Map `state` values for your problem workflow; (2) Add filters for known backlog categories excluded from SLA; (3) Weekly review of `max_age` with problem managers and link to related `snow:incident` volume via `problem_id` in a companion panel.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:problem" earliest=-180d
| where NOT match(lower(state),"(?i)closed|resolved")
| eval age_days=round((now()-sys_created_on)/86400,0)
| stats count as open_pr, median(age_days) as med_age, max(age_days) as max_age by assignment_group, category
| sort -open_pr
```

Understanding this SPL

**Open Problem Record Aging** ‚Äî Aging open problems indicate stalled root-cause work and undermine permanent corrective actions; backlog visibility strengthens ITIL problem management cadence with incident and change stakeholders.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:problem"` (`state`, `sys_created_on`, `assignment_group`, `category`, `number`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:problem. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:problem", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where NOT match(lower(state),"(?i)closed|resolved")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by assignment_group, category** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with the ServiceNow list or form that matches this data (for example the incident, request, or problem view). Reconcile a few record numbers, states, and times. Confirm the add-on poll or export time lines up with what you see in the browser.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (open problem aging by group), Bar chart (open count by category), Single value (total open problems).
grandmaExplanation: We surface problem records that sit open, so the organization either drives root-cause work or says why the problem stays open on purpose.
